description_long: This is a detailed, painted sculpture of a seated deity or spiritual figure, likely inspired by Buddhist or Hindu iconography. The figure is adorned with an elaborate headdress, jewelry, and flowing garments, and sits cross-legged on a round, ornate pedestal. Four arms are visible, each holding symbolic objects or posed in traditional mudras, and the facial expression is serene and meditative. The sculpture features a rich palette of colors, including gold, green, red, and cream, with intricate detailing on the clothing and base. The surface appears hard and slightly glossy, suggesting it is made from painted ceramic or cast metal. The overall craftsmanship is intricate, with careful attention to ornamental details and posture.
description: A richly painted, ornate sculpture of a seated deity with four arms, elaborate headdress, and detailed garments, sitting on a round pedestal. The figure exudes a serene, meditative presence with intricate decorative elements.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the back of the headdress, flowing hair, and the ornate back of the pedestal, with arms and decorative elements extending outward.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the face, the layered garments, and the side details of the arms and pedestal.
description_view_2: Frontal view displaying the serene face, four arms with symbolic gestures, elaborate chest jewelry, and the full decorative base.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the profile of the face, arm positions, and the intricate side details of the headdress and pedestal.
attribute_subject_matter: seated deity or spiritual figure with four arms
attribute_location_context: No visible environmental context; background is isolated and does not indicate installation setting.
